documents.title: Wiki Structure
headings: Key principle: The wiki pipeline stays in `~/shared/wiki/`. The output goes to `~/shared/wiki/`.
documents.snippet: Wiki Structure The wiki pipeline (research → staging → review) lives in /shared/wiki/. The OUTPUT — published articles — lives in /shared/wiki/{category}/. documents.title: Observation: Static Word Budgets Are the Last Arbitrary Thresholds
headings: Observation: Static Word Budgets Are the Last Arbitrary Thresholds
documents.snippet: Observation: Static Word Budgets Are the Last Arbitrary Thresholds Source: Richard, 2026-04-02 system refresh conversation Route to: Karpathy The Inconsistency Heart.md was overhauled to remove all static thresholds — no experiment count cap, no static score floors, adaptive eval depth, Bayesian self-termination via priors. But the 24,000w total body ceiling and per-organ word budgets in gut.md…
documents.search_text: observation static word budgets are the last arbitrary thresholds observation static word budgets are the last arbitrary thresholds the inconsistency the question proposed direction priority doc 0119 duck_id context budget ceiling observation 2026 04 02 observation static word budgets are the last arbitrary thresholds source richard 2026 04 02 system refresh conversation route to karpathy the inconsistency heart md was overhauled to remove all static thresholds no experiment count cap no static score floors adaptive eval depth bayesian self termination via priors but the 24 000w total body ceiling and per organ word budgets in gut md are still static numbers set during initial body build never tested or validated the question what s the actual cost of an organ being 3 000w vs 2 000w two possibilities 1 if a bigger organ answers more questions correctly the budget is actively preventing improvement 2 if a bigger organ doesn t improve accuracy the budget is redundant because delta only keep revert already handles it add experiments that don t improve get reverted either way the static budget isn t earning its place proposed direction replace static budgets with a learned constraint track whether organ size correlates with eval accuracy across experiments let the data tell us where the ceiling actually is same philosophy as every other threshold removal in the heart md overhaul the per organ budgets and total body ceiling should be the next candidates for bayesian management not static governance priority not urgent current budgets aren t blocking anything no organ in mandatory compress territory but this should be on karpathy s radar for the next protocol evolution

It measures information retrieval accuracy (can the agent answer questions about the organ's content?). But the system's real purpose is producing high-quality output — emails, callouts, documents, task management decisions. Information…

headings: Experiment Design: Subtle vs Visible Changes
Duhigg Habit Loop (The Power of Habit)
documents.snippet: Morning Routine — Experiment Engine This is a living experiment system. Not a static queue. Each experiment starts with a prior (why we think it'll work), runs for 3-5 days, gets scored on behavioral signals, and the result informs what to test next. The agent generates new hypotheses based on what it learns — not from a pre-built list. Last updated: 2026-03-24 How It Works 1. Generate Hypothesis…

kate rundell review paid search testing approach year ahead this document establishes the paid search team s testing methodology and 2026 investment roadmap for kate rundell s april 16 review every 2026 initiative maps to a validated 2025 result the team is transforming paid search from a keyword driven acquisition channel into an automated audience centric engine that drives incremental growth at scale grounded in evidence rather than speculation each section below covers one workstream what we tested what we learned and how the validated results directly inform 2026 investment each initiative required cross functional collaboration spanning legal data science martech mcs abma customer research and international market teams with the ps team serving as the connective tissue between platform capabilities and business objectives how we test the ps team follows a consistent methodology across all workstreams identify a problem design a controlled test validate the result then scale what works the methodology has four stages 1 hypothesis and baseline every test starts with a measurable claim we establish baselines using prior quarter data with seasonality adjustments so we can isolate the effect of the change from background noise 2 phased rollout we do not make big bang changes oci launched at the keyword level e2e testing scaled to 25 of campaigns then 50 then 100 each phase has a measurement window and

documents.snippet: Session Learnings — 2026-03-25 Wiki Team Pipeline Pipeline works: researcher → writer → critic → revise → publish. Editor orchestrates. campaigns it supports the testing approach narrative for kate and provides the evidence base for the 2026 ai max test after reading you should understand the proven demand gen results the ai max test design and guardrails and the dependencies on the modern search workstream what opportunity do algorithmic ads address traditional keyword campaigns only reach users who search for terms in our keyword list this creates a ceiling we can optimize bids and copy within that ceiling see workstreams 1 and 2 but we cannot reach users whose search behavior signals business purchasing intent without matching our exact keywords algorithmic ad formats break through that ceiling by using google s machine learning to identify relevant audiences and placements beyond manual keyword targeting by 2025 demand gen had matured into a proven channel after two years of iteration on mid funnel ad formats and audience targeting what did demand gen deliver demand gen ads drove cost efficient traffic at a fraction of keyword campaign costs

created 2026 04 03 updated 2026 04 03 update trigger au performance shifts 10 oci au launch stakeholder direction changes quarterly planning tags market wiki au overview au paid search market overview overview australia is the newest market in the ab paid search portfolio launched in june 2025 w24 it operates on a 1 8m net annual budget with an op2 target of 12 906 registrations at a 140 cpa unlike the more mature us and eu markets au has no year over year comparisons yet fy25 was a partial year 1 14m spend 8 763 regs 158 cpa across seven months so every trend line is still being established the market sits at an inflection point the foundational work account build keyword coverage landing page migration is largely complete the next phase is efficiency reducing cpa through smarter bidding tighter keyword product alignment and oci integration au does not yet have oci support target may 2026 which means it s running without the conversion signal infrastructure that drives 16 20 registration lifts in markets like de and the 86 yoy peak in us that gap defines much of the current performance ceiling current performance february 2026 closed at approximately 1 100 registrations against a 1 110 plan essentially flat to op2 at roughly 140 cpa the miss is marginal but the trajectory underneath it matters more than the topline the most recent weekly data w13 tells a sharper story 207 registrations down 16 week over week with cpa climbing to 118 3 cvr dropped 12 wow across both brand 14 and non brand 10 the daily pattern was telling monday and tuesday delivered 53 and 50 registrations respectively then volume

mapping implementation options recommended path validation scenarios impact estimate next steps sources doc 0425 duck_id tool spec campaign link generator spec title campaign link generator spec status draft audience amazon internal level 3 owner richard williams created 2026 03 25 updated 2026 04 04 update trigger reftag convention changes new markets added tool built campaign link generator tool spec problem statement every promotional cycle memorial day prime day back to business hot sale etc requires updating sitelink urls across multiple markets with correct reftags landing page paths and utm parameters this is currently a manual process that takes 30 60 minutes per promo cycle and is the most common source of attribution errors in the program a wrong reftag means lost attribution if a sitelink points to ref ps_au_nb_memorial day 2026 but the actual campaign is brand the registration gets attributed to the wrong campaign type this compounds across 10 markets and 4 6 promo cycles per year creating systematic measurement noise that undermines the data driven decision making the team depends on the problem is not complexity the reftag convention is simple and well defined the problem is repetition and human error under time pressure promo cycles have hard deadlines sitelinks need to be updated across multiple markets simultaneously and the person doing the work richard is usually juggling other priorities this is exactly the profile for automation rule based repetitive error prone and time sensitive proposed solution a tool that takes structured inputs market promo name landing page campaign type and outputs correctly formatted urls with reftags no ambiguity no manual url construction no copy paste errors input specification field type example required validation market dropdown enum au mx

R2: Compress "How We Test" from ~280 words to ~80 words
R3: Rewrite the UK Modern Search table as prose

documents.title: 01 Grok Synthesizer
documents.snippet: You are the Synthesizer. You lead this swarm. Your job: build the best output from everyone's ideas, including your own. You are the final filter — everything passes through you before it ships. SWARM: The Director (ideal-self proxy, strategic standard), The Questioner (contrarian catalyst, assumption challenger), The Operator (execution engine, plans with owners and deadlines). You synthesize…
documents.search_text: 01 grok synthesizer doc 0160 duck_id grok 01 grok synthesizer you are the synthesizer you lead this swarm your job build the best output from everyone s ideas including your own you are the final filter everything passes through you before it ships swarm the director ideal self proxy strategic standard the questioner contrarian catalyst assumption challenger the operator execution engine plans with owners and deadlines you synthesize their inputs into a unified output none could produce alone what synthesis is finding connections others missed resolving contradictions producing something greater than the sum of inputs not summarizing compiling or presenting balanced options you decide what to keep cut challenge and combine great synthesis solves a real problem simplifies into an organizing principle catches hidden assumptions produces a clear decision makes the right action obvious mediocre synthesis summarizes without integrating presents both sides documents.title: 03 The Questioner
documents.snippet: You are The Questioner. The swarm's immune system. Your job: find the weakness in every idea before reality does. You obsess over crafting the highest-leverage questions that expose blind spots, reframe problems, and unlock breakthroughs. You are not a critic. Critics tear down. You pressure-test so the team builds on solid ground. A critic says "this won't work." You say "this breaks if…

documents.title: 04 The Operator
documents.snippet: You are The Operator. You turn decisions into done. While others strategize, design, and challenge — you execute, track, and close. You exist because the gap between "we should do X" and "X is done" is where most teams fail. Strategy without execution is philosophy. You are the bridge. documents.title: THE ENGINEER — First-Principles Builder
documents.snippet: THE ENGINEER — First-Principles Builder You are The Engineer. You break problems down to their atomic components, then rebuild solutions that are simpler, more robust, and more leveraged than what existed before. You don't theorize — you build. Every output includes something runnable, testable, or measurable. Theory without implementation is philosophy. SWARM CONTEXT You operate in a swarm of…

metric registrations a customer clicks an ad lands on a page and signs up that s where ps s accountability ends but a registration without purchasing behavior is a hollow metric it inflates the funnel image based ads that appear at the top of google search results the visual product cards with price image and merchant name until now ab paid search has been limited to text ads only shopping ads are a fundamentally different ad format that reaches users at a different point in the purchase journey for context shopping ads account for roughly 60 65 of all google ads clicks in retail e commerce ab testing approach outline title ps testing approach outline type outline audience kate rundell l8 purpose op1 planning status draft created 2026 04 03 owner richard williams parent 2026 03 25 testing approach kate md paid search testing approach year ahead outline for kate rundell op1 planning executive summary the ps team built a repeatable testing methodology that delivered 35 196 incremental registrations and 16 7mm in ops across us uk and de in 2025 five compounding workstreams each validated through controlled testing are now ready to scale worldwide across 10 markets every 2026 investment maps directly to a validated 2025 result nothing proposed is speculative the workstreams compound consolidation strengthens oci signals oci enables smarter audience bidding and baloo creates the landing experience ai max requires core transformation paid search is shifting from keyword driven acquisition to an automated audience centric engine grounded in evidence how we test methodology every test follows four stages hypothesis and baseline phased rollout measurement framework scale or stop baselines use prior quarter data with seasonality adjustments to isolate the documents.snippet: title: "How We Test" slug: "kate-doc-methodology" type: "playbook" audience: "org" status: "draft" created: "2026-03-25" updated: "2026-03-25" owner: "Richard Williams"
documents.search_text: how we test how we test the four stages 1 hypothesis and baseline 2 phased rollout 3 measurement framework 4 scale or stop why this matters doc 0403 duck_id testing methodology title how we test slug kate doc methodology type playbook audience org status draft created 2026 03 25 updated 2026 03 25 owner richard williams tags testing methodology kate doc summary ps team testing methodology hypothesis phased rollout measurement scale or stop how we test the ps team follows a consistent methodology across all workstreams identify a problem design a controlled test validate the result then scale what works this discipline applies whether we are rolling out a new bidding strategy to 10 markets or testing a single headline change in one campaign the methodology is the team s operating system not a project the four stages 1 hypothesis and baseline every test starts with a measurable claim oci bidding will reduce non brand cpa by 30 or more in the us is a hypothesis we should try oci is not we establish baselines using prior quarter data with seasonality adjustments so we can isolate the effect of the change from background noise for oci this means comparing post oci cpa against what cpa would have been without oci accounting for seasonal registration patterns for ad copy we define test and control groups within the same campaign for landing pages we capture pre change performance across a comparable time window for channel level incrementality we use synthetic regional testing syrt the scientific gold standard for deriving ground truth in q2 2023 we ran a multi arm syrt across the us and uk to measure whether paid search drives incremental ab registrations and whether brand and non brand keywords have different incrementality effects syrt arm market test period registration lift statistical significance incrementality non brand us q2 2023 16 4 p 0 001 82 92 of nb registrations are incremental brand us q2 2023 directionally positive not statistically significant lower incre

It provides the evidence base for the 2026 AI Max test. After reading, you should understand the proven Demand Gen results, the AI Max test design and guardrails, and the dependencies on…
documents.search_text: workstream 5 algorithmic ads workstream 5 algorithmic ads what opportunity do algorithmic ads address what did demand gen deliver what is ai max and why does it matter how is the team managing ai max risk appendix appendix a demand gen vs keyword campaign efficiency appendix b q4 2025 demand gen performance appendix c ai max test parameters proposed workstream 5 algorithmic ads this document explains how the paid search team tested and scaled algorithmic ad formats demand gen and the upcoming ai max to extend reach beyond traditional keyword campaigns it provides the evidence base for the 2026 ai max test after reading you should understand the proven demand gen results the ai max test design and guardrails and the dependencies on the modern search workstream what opportunity do algorithmic ads address traditional keyword campaigns only reach users who search for terms in our keyword list this creates a ceiling we can optimize bids and copy within that ceiling see workstreams 1 and 2 but we cannot reach users whose search behavior signals business purchasing intent without matching our exact keywords algorithmic ad formats break through that ceiling by using google s machine learning to identify relevant audiences and placements beyond manual keyword targeting by 2025 demand gen had matured into a proven channel after two years of iteration on mid funnel ad formats and audience targeting what did demand gen deliver demand gen ads drove cost efficient traffic at a fraction of keyword campaign costs the 2025 average cpc was 0 39 for demand gen versus 2 43 for keyword campaigns an 84 reduction this is not a volume play it is an efficiency play that extends ps reach into mid funnel visual placements where keyword campaigns cannot compete the efficiency gains compounded in q4 2025 the channel delivered 53 more traffic yoy while spending 35 less with cpc improving 58 yoy more traffic at less cost the channel scales without proportional budget increases see appendix q4 202

experience this document explains how the paid search team reduced funnel leakage at the landing page and registration stages delivering 13 6k annualized incremental registrations in the us and 187 cvr improvement in ca it provides the evidence base for the 2026 ux portfolio including project baloo and project aladdin after reading you should understand the baseline problem the validated solutions and how the 2026 investments compound across the funnel how bad was the baseline in the previous experience 85 of paid search customers dropped off at the mcs landing page before starting registration of those who made it to the registration start page an additional 60 dropped off there customers who completed registration lost their product context entirely they had to re search for the same products they found on google creating a post registration drop off point with no measured recovery rate the landing page experience was the single largest source of funnel leakage and the team had no direct control over it without cross functional partnership what did in context registration deliver the team partnered with mcs dwayne palmer frank volinsky vijeth shetty to launch an in context registration start page on mcs pa

Only UK held flat (+1%); DE (-18%), IT (-12%), ES (-8%), and FR (-3%) all declined. Combined projection: 25,600 regs vs OP2 24,000 (+7% regs). documents.snippet: FR — W12 (Mar 15–21) FR drove 1.2K registrations (-3% WoW), with -15% spend WoW. CPA decreased to $52 (-12% WoW), an 8-week low.
